name: Zero Trust Network Access
strategicIntent: Enforce Zero Trust principles across all network boundaries, ensuring every access request is verified regardless of source location.
problemStatement: Traditional perimeter-based security is insufficient for hybrid cloud environments. Implicit trust within the corporate network exposes McCain to lateral movement attacks.

useCases.use: Any production workload accessing corporate resources
useCases.dontUse: Isolated dev sandbox environments with no corporate data
Air-gapped OT networks with no internet connectivity

components: Azure AD Conditional Access — context-aware access decisions
Microsoft Defender for Cloud — continuous security posture assessment
Azure AD Privileged Identity Management — just-in-time admin access
Microsoft Sentinel — SIEM and threat detection
interactionSteps: User authenticates via Azure AD with MFA
Conditional Access evaluates device compliance and risk signals
PIM activates just-in-time role assignment if privileged access requested
Defender for Cloud continuously monitors workload security posture
Sentinel correlates security events for threat detection
securityConsiderations: All access requests must be authenticated and authorized
Network micro-segmentation must be enforced at all layers
Device compliance checks required before resource access
Privileged access requires just-in-time activation via PIM
All authentication events logged to Sentinel

guardrails.rule: MFA required for all privileged access
Device compliance check mandatory for corporate resource access
All authentication events must be logged

exceptionProcess: Submit security exception via ServiceNow with CISO approval. Document compensating controls and set 90-day review cadence.

revisionHistory.author: Sarah Chen
revisionHistory.summary: Initial draft for Architecture Board review
